settings_item_auth_cookie_secure_desc: Controls whether auth and share-verification cookies are only sent over HTTPS. You can disable it for temporary plain-HTTP internal testing, but it should stay enabled in production.
settings_item_auth_allow_user_registrati: Controls whether new users can create accounts from the public sign-up flow. When disabled, only the initial setup flow and admin-created accounts remain available.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_e: Controls whether new users must confirm their email address before they can sign in for the first time.
settings_item_auth_local_email_allowlist: Email addresses or exact ASCII domains allowed for local sign-up and local email changes. Leave empty to allow any email that is not blocked. This does not apply to third-party SSO. Internationalized domains must use punycode.
settings_item_auth_local_email_blocklist: Email addresses or exact ASCII domains blocked for local sign-up and local email changes. Blocklist entries override the allowlist. This does not apply to third-party SSO. Internationalized domains must use punycode.
settings_item_auth_access_token_ttl_secs: Controls how long newly issued access tokens stay valid after login or refresh.
settings_item_auth_refresh_token_ttl_sec: Controls how long refresh tokens stay valid before users must sign in again.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_t: Controls how long registration activation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_change_ttl_se: Controls how long email change confirmation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_ttl_se: Controls how long password reset links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_verification_: The minimum wait between verification email resend attempts for the same user.
settings_item_auth_email_code_login_enab: When enabled and SMTP mail is configured, verified-email users without TOTP must enter an 8-digit email code after the password or external sign-in step. Configure the SMTP host and sender address in Mail Settings first; SMTP username and password must both be filled or both left blank.
settings_item_auth_passkey_login_enabled: Controls whether users can complete login with registered passkeys. Disabling it does not delete existing passkeys; they can be used again after this setting is re-enabled.
email_code_mfa_mail_config_required: Complete mail delivery settings first: SMTP host and sender address are required, and SMTP username and password must both be filled or both left blank.
settings_item_auth_email_code_login_allo: Lets users who already have TOTP use email code as an additional login challenge method. Keep this off if email should not weaken authenticator-app MFA.
settings_item_auth_email_code_login_ttl_: The maximum lifetime of an issued email login code. The actual lifetime never exceeds the remaining MFA challenge time.
settings_item_auth_email_code_login_rese: The minimum wait before the same user can request another email login code.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_reques: The minimum wait between password reset email requests for the same user.
settings_item_default_storage_quota_desc: The default storage quota assigned to newly created users. Use 0 for unlimited.
settings_item_gravatar_base_url_desc: The base URL used to build Gravatar avatar requests. Replace it with a reachable proxy or mirror if the default service is inaccessible.
